mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or positive returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 1.0pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable or falling federal funds rates indicate strong consumer demand supported by accommodative policy, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when retail sales growth exceeds 5% but rates remain unchanged, or interaction term IC is below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects supply chain bottlenecks and reduced corporate output, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 70% of declining industrial production periods within the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects expanding economic activity and corporate earnings potential, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window or positive returns occur in less than 55% of high-GDP periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls alongside increasing housing starts signal synchronized labor and housing market expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in more than 55% of cases or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant with p greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates contracting manufacturing activity and reduced corporate cash flows, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 65% of instances where industrial production falls during tightening periods.
